steps.title: Confirm scene safety
steps.description: Confirm scene is safe. Identify mechanism of injury: MVA, fall, penetrating, blast, burn, crush. Note environmental hazards and ensure adequate lighting.

steps.title: Assess for entrapment/extrication needs
steps.description: Determine if patient is trapped or requires extrication. If entrapped: stabilise vehicle/structure, protect patient from further harm, contact fire/rescue if needed. Do NOT attempt extrication beyond your training.

steps.title: Primary survey (ABCDE approach)
steps.description: Rapid structured assessment: Airway (with manual C-spine stabilisation), Breathing (look, listen, feel), Circulation (pulse, skin colour, capillary refill, bleeding), Disability (GCS, pupils, limb movement), Exposure (remove clothing to identify injuries).

steps.title: Manual in-line stabilisation
steps.description: Maintain manual in-line cervical spine stabilisation from initial contact. Apply rigid cervical collar when available and log-roll to place on spinal board.

steps.title: Control external haemorrhage
steps.description: Apply direct pressure to bleeding wounds. Use haemostatic dressings for deep wounds. Apply tourniquet for life-threatening extremity haemorrhage (note time of application). Pack junctional wounds.

steps.title: Record initial vitals
steps.description: Record: RR, SpO2, BP, HR, consciousness (AVPU/GCS), temperature, capillary refill. Note mechanism details: height of fall, speed of impact, intrusion depth, restraint use.

steps.title: Establish IV/IO access
steps.description: Two large-bore peripheral IV lines. If unable to obtain IV within 90 seconds, establish IO access. Begin warmed crystalloid per local trauma protocol.

steps.title: Splint suspected fractures
steps.description: Splint all suspected long bone and joint fractures. Check distal pulses, sensation, and motor function before and after splinting. Document neurovascular status.

steps.title: Reassess vitals (every 5 min)
steps.description: Repeat vital signs every 5 minutes during transport. Document trend. Alert receiving facility of deterioration.

steps.title: Determine disposition
steps.description: Record destination facility, transport mode, and trauma team notification status. Ensure receiving facility has: mechanism details, injury pattern, vital sign trend, and treatments given.
